text: Here, I wander through a dimly lit corridor, glass reflecting sterile ceiling lights. The quiet is thick, broken only by the distant hum of fluorescents. I feel a strange sense of anticipation, as if I’m trespassing in a zone reserved for work and ideas, now dormant after hours. The presence of emergency signs and equipment suggests preparedness, yet the emptiness makes me alert, almost uneasy—as though meaning floats in the silence, waiting to be found.
